pains.summary: Current attribution model only uses first-touch based on Salesforce campaign events and doesn't include online digital touches
pains.quote: we have currently, we have an attribution model where we do like a look back and it's based on first touch. Okay. That's basically just all based on Salesforce campaign events. It doesn't include like the online digital touches just yet.
pains.speaker: Chethan Chandra

pains.summary: Cannot stitch together digital activity with later sales conversations to understand full customer journey
pains.quote: if there is activity on our web through our content properties, and then they have a conversation with sales like six months later, I want to be able to stitch that experience together.
pains.speaker: Chethan Chandra

pains.summary: Need to de-anonymize website visitors based on cookies/tags to capture pre-sales signals
pains.quote: whatever we can de anonymize based on like a munchkin cookie or some kind of tag. Yeah. I wanna be able to capture that.
pains.speaker: Chethan Chandra

pains.summary: Digital spend attribution is difficult with manual data stitching required between Salesforce and BigQuery
pains.quote: We have to ingest the Salesforce data into BigQuery and then do all the stitching. We do all of that.
pains.speaker: Chethan Chandra

pains.summary: Campaign hierarchy and structure housekeeping needed in Salesforce and Google
pains.quote: there's some housekeeping we have to do like campaign hierarchy, campaign structure on our sales force, like Google. We spend a million dollars on Google and here's the parent campaign, children campaigns.
pains.speaker: Chethan Chandra

tools_switched_from.reason: Currently using for digital spend attribution but requires manual data stitching and BigQuery integration
tools_switched_from.quote: we use tools like, or I, in, historically we've used tools like Imprivado, Datarama, where it actually calls APIs to like, you know, our ad exchange vendors like Google, LinkedIn, SEM Meta. Yeah. And it brings it into like, like a big query database, and then we connect it to our Salesforce campaigns to measure like ROII
tools_switched_from.speaker: Chethan Chandra

tools_switched_from.reason: Using for web analytics but cannot stitch with CRM data for full customer journey
tools_switched_from.quote: we use GA four, you know, so for example, if there's a Google click parameter that we can ident as a unique identifier for, for me, like if I'm looking at the website
tools_switched_from.speaker: Chethan Chandra

contrarians.claim: Click and impression metrics are 'false positives' for measuring digital spend performance
contrarians.why_unusual: Most digital marketers still rely heavily on clicks and impressions as primary metrics; explicitly rejecting them as misleading is contrarian
contrarians.quote: Click and impressions and clicks are too, it doesn't, it's, yeah, it's very, it's a false positive.
contrarians.speaker: Chethan Chandra

best_about_revsure.summary: RevSure's propensity engine and stitching ability are valuable for Zscaler's use cases
best_about_revsure.quote: I think there's some value add here based on the propensity engine that you have that's powering this data. And the stitch stitching ability.
best_about_revsure.speaker: Chethan Chandra

best_about_revsure.summary: RevSure's flexibility in defining conversion metrics and ROI measurement
best_about_revsure.speaker: Chethan Chandra

long_term.win: End-to-end customer journey visualization with ability to apply scoring on touches
long_term.quote: that gives me like basically as close to, we can get like an end to end customer journey. And then I want to be able to apply scoring on that, on those touches.
long_term.speaker: Chethan Chandra

long_term.win: Data science work for predictive scoring
long_term.quote: there's some data science work there that we need to do as well.
long_term.speaker: Chethan Chandra

before_state.pain_summary: First-touch attribution based only on Salesforce campaign events, with digital and offline activities siloed; manual stitching required for spend ROI analysis
